  FiniteDifference.laxFriedrichsStep_abs_le cfl
    (FiniteDifference.laxFriedrichsStep cfl u0 u1)
    (FiniteDifference.laxFriedrichsStep cfl u1 u2) M hcflLower hcflUpper hinner01 hinner12
have hright : |FiniteDifference.laxFriedrichsStep cfl
    (FiniteDifference.laxFriedrichsStep cfl u1 u2)
    (FiniteDifference.laxFriedrichsStep cfl u2 u3)| ≤ M :=
  FiniteDifference.laxFriedrichsStep_abs_le cfl

    (FiniteDifference.laxFriedrichsStep cfl u2 u3) M hcflLower hcflUpper hinner12 hinner23
have hweights := FiniteDifference.laxFriedrichsWeights_nonneg cfl hcflLower hcflUpper
have hwsum := FiniteDifference.laxFriedrichsWeights_sum cfl
set wL := FiniteDifference.laxFriedrichsLeftWeight cfl with hwL
set wR := FiniteDifference.laxFriedrichsRightWeight cfl with hwR
set L := FiniteDifference.laxFriedrichsStep cfl
    (FiniteDifference.laxFriedrichsStep cfl u0 u1)
    (FiniteDifference.laxFriedrichsStep cfl u1 u2) with hL

    (FiniteDifference.laxFriedrichsStep cfl u2 u3) with hR
obtain ⟨hwLnn, hwRnn⟩ := hweights
calc |wL * L + wR * R| ≤ |wL * L| + |wR * R| := abs_add_le _ _
  _ = wL * |L| + wR * |R| := by

        apply add_le_add
        · exact mul_le_mul_of_nonneg_left hleft hwLnn
        · exact mul_le_mul_of_nonneg_left hright hwRnn
  _ = (wL + wR) * M := by ring
  _ = M := by rw [hwsum]; ring

· apply FiniteDifference.upwindStep_abs_le cfl _ _ M hcfl0 hcfl1
  · apply FiniteDifference.upwindStep_abs_le cfl _ _ M hcfl0 hcfl1 hMinus h0
  · apply FiniteDifference.upwindStep_abs_le cfl _ _ M hcfl0 hcfl1 h0 h1
· apply FiniteDifference.upwindStep_abs_le cfl _ _ M hcfl0 hcfl1
  · apply FiniteDifference.upwindStep_abs_le cfl _ _ M hcfl0 hcfl1 h0 h1
  · apply FiniteDifference.upwindStep_abs_le cfl _ _ M hcfl0 hcfl1 h1 h2

rw [FiniteDifference.discreteMass_kdvCanonicalUpdate_of_boundary_eq scale _ n hflux1]
exact FiniteDifference.discreteMass_kdvCanonicalUpdate_of_boundary_eq scale u n hflux0

The sums are `totalOn`. Let me rewrite using `totalOn` and the canonical update theorems.

```
have h1 : FiniteDifference.totalOn
    (fun i => FiniteDifference.kdvCanonicalUpdate scale
      (fun j => FiniteDifference.kdvCanonicalUpdate scale u j) i) n =
    FiniteDifference.totalOn
      (fun j => FiniteDifference.kdvCanonicalUpdate scale u j) n :=
  FiniteDifference.totalOn_kdvCanonicalUpdate_of_boundary_eq scale
    (fun j => FiniteDifference.kdvCanonicalUpdate scale u j) n hflux1
have h2 : FiniteDifference.totalOn
    (fun i => FiniteDifference.kdvCanonicalUpdate scale u i) n =
    FiniteDifference.totalOn u n :=
  FiniteDifference.totalOn_kdvCanonicalUpdate_of_boundary_eq scale u n hflux0
have e1 : Finset.sum (Finset.range n)
    (fun i => FiniteDifference.kdvCanonicalUpdate scale
      (fun j => FiniteDifference.kdvCanonicalUpdate scale u j) i) =
    FiniteDifference.totalOn
      (fun i => FiniteDifference.kdvCanonicalUpdate scale
        (fun j => FiniteDifference.kdvCanonicalUpdate scale u j) i) n := rfl
have e2 : Finset.sum (Finset.range n) (fun i => u i) =
    FiniteDifference.totalOn u n := rfl
rw [e1, e2, h1, h2]
ring
```

rw [FEM.weightedP0Projection_orthogonal_const s m u c hmass]
rw [FEM.weightedP0Projection_orthogonal_const s m v c hmass]
ring
exact FEM.weightedP0Projection_orthogonal_const s m (fun j => u j + v j) c hmass

The sum `∑ i ∈ s, a i * (constant)` factors as `(∑ a i) * constant = constant`. The constant is `finiteRowApply s b x`, whose abs is bounded by M.

```
have hconst : Finset.sum s (fun i => a i * LinearAlgebra.finiteRowApply s b x) = LinearAlgebra.finiteRowApply s b x := by
  rw [← Finset.sum_mul, hsumA, one_mul]
rw [hconst]
exact LinearAlgebra.finiteRowApply_abs_le s b x M hb hsumB hx
```

apply LinearAlgebra.finiteRowApply_abs_le s a (fun _ => LinearAlgebra.finiteRowApply s b x) M ha hsumA
intro i hi
exact LinearAlgebra.finiteRowApply_abs_le s b x M hb hsumB hx

apply LinearAlgebra.row2Apply_abs_le a b _ _ M ha hb hab
· apply LinearAlgebra.row2Apply_abs_le c d _ _ M hc hd hcd
  · apply LinearAlgebra.row2Apply_abs_le g h x0 x1 M hg hh hgh hx0 hx1
  · apply LinearAlgebra.row2Apply_abs_le p q x1 x2 M hp hq hpq hx1 hx2
· apply LinearAlgebra.row2Apply_abs_le e f _ _ M he hf hef
  · apply LinearAlgebra.row2Apply_abs_le p q x1 x2 M hp hq hpq hx1 hx2
  · apply LinearAlgebra.row2Apply_abs_le r s x2 x3 M hr hs hrs hx2 hx3

Looking at this, I need to bound the outermost `rowStochastic2First a b`. I'll repeatedly apply the bound lemmas building up from inner expressions.

```lean
  have h01 : |LinearAlgebra.rowStochastic2First g h x0 x1| <= M :=
    (LinearAlgebra.rowStochastic2_infNorm_abs_le g h g h x0 x1 M hg hh hg hh hgh hgh hx0 hx1).1
  have h12 : |LinearAlgebra.rowStochastic2First p q x1 x2| <= M :=
    (LinearAlgebra.rowStochastic2_infNorm_abs_le p q p q x1 x2 M hp hq hp hq hpq hpq hx1 hx2).1
  have h23 : |LinearAlgebra.rowStochastic2First r s x2 x3| <= M :=
    (LinearAlgebra.rowStochastic2_infNorm_abs_le r s r s x2 x3 M hr hs hr hs hrs hrs hx2 hx3).1
  have hcD : |LinearAlgebra.rowStochastic2First c d
        (LinearAlgebra.rowStochastic2First g h x0 x1)
        (LinearAlgebra.rowStochastic2First p q x1 x2)| <= M :=
    (LinearAlgebra.rowStochastic2_infNorm_abs_le c d c d
      (LinearAlgebra.rowStochastic2First g h x0 x1)
      (LinearAlgebra.rowStochastic2First p q x1 x2) M
      hc hd hc hd hcd hcd h01 h12).1
  have heF : |LinearAlgebra.rowStochastic2First e f
        (LinearAlgebra.rowStochastic2First p q x1 x2)
        (LinearAlgebra.rowStochastic2First r s x2 x3)| <= M :=
    (LinearAlgebra.rowStochastic2_infNorm_abs_le e f e f
      (LinearAlgebra.rowStochastic2First p q x1 x2)
      (LinearAlgebra.rowStochastic2First r s x2 x3) M
      he hf he hf hef hef h12 h23).1
  exact (LinearAlgebra.rowStochastic2_infNorm_abs_le a b a b
    (LinearAlgebra.rowStochastic2First c d
      (LinearAlgebra.rowStochastic2First g h x0 x1)
      (LinearAlgebra.rowStochastic2First p q x1 x2))
    (LinearAlgebra.rowStochastic2First e f
      (LinearAlgebra.rowStochastic2First p q x1 x2)
      (LinearAlgebra.rowStochastic2First r s x2 x3)) M
    ha hb ha hb hab hab hcD heF).1
```
